name: Migrate a JavaScript Project to TypeScript
description: Convert an existing JavaScript project to TypeScript incrementally by renaming files, adding types, and enabling strict mode over time.
trendingReason: JavaScript-to-TypeScript migration is the most requested developer workflow as teams adopt type safety

steps.title: Install TypeScript and generate tsconfig
steps.description: Add TypeScript and create a permissive initial configuration that allows JavaScript files.
steps.tip: Start with strict: false and allowJs: true to avoid hundreds of errors on day one.

steps.title: Configure tsconfig for gradual migration
steps.description: Set allowJs and checkJs to enable mixed JS/TS development.
steps.command:
cat > tsconfig.json << 'EOF'
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowJs": true,
    "checkJs": false,
    "strict": false,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
EOF
steps.warning: Do not enable strict: true at the start. Migrate files first, then enable strict flags incrementally.

steps.title: Rename files from .js to .ts incrementally
steps.description: Start with utility files and leaf modules that have no dependents.
steps.tip: Rename leaf files first (utilities, constants, types) then work inward to core modules and entry points.

steps.title: Install type definitions for dependencies
steps.description: Add @types packages for libraries that do not include their own types.

steps.title: Run type checking and fix errors
steps.description: Incrementally fix type errors file by file.
steps.tip: Use 'any' as an escape hatch for complex types initially, then refine to proper types in a follow-up pass.

steps.title: Enable strict flags after all files are converted
steps.description: Once all .js files are renamed to .ts, enable strict mode incrementally.
steps.command: npx tsc --noEmit --strict 2>&1 | grep -c 'error TS' && echo 'errors remaining'

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# JavaScript to TypeScript Migration
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Installing TypeScript ==="
pnpm add -D typescript @types/node

echo ""
echo "=== Migration Progress ==="
JS_COUNT=$(find src -name '*.js' -o -name '*.jsx' | wc -l)
TS_COUNT=$(find src -name '*.ts' -o -name '*.tsx' | wc -l)
TOTAL=$((JS_COUNT + TS_COUNT))
if [ $TOTAL -gt 0 ]; then
  PCT=$((TS_COUNT * 100 / TOTAL))
else
  PCT=0
fi

echo "JavaScript files: $JS_COUNT"
echo "TypeScript files: $TS_COUNT"
echo "Migration progress: $PCT%"

echo ""
echo "=== Type Errors ==="
ERRORS=$(npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -c 'error TS' || true)
echo "Current type errors: $ERRORS"

echo ""
echo "=== Migration Steps ==="
echo "1. Rename leaf files (.js -> .ts) first"
echo "2. Add types to function parameters and return values"
echo "3. Install @types/ packages for untyped dependencies"
echo "4. Replace 'any' with proper types"
echo "5. Enable strict flags one at a time"
echo "6. Enable strict: true when all flags pass"

faq.question: Can I use TypeScript and JavaScript files side by side?
faq.answer: Yes. Set allowJs: true in tsconfig.json. TypeScript can import from JavaScript files and vice versa. This is the key to incremental migration without blocking the team.

faq.question: How long does a typical migration take?
faq.answer: It depends on project size. A 50-file project might take 1-2 weeks. A 500-file project might take 2-3 months. The key is migrating incrementally without blocking feature development. Rename a few files per PR.

faq.question: Should I use JSDoc types instead of converting to .ts files?
faq.answer: JSDoc types work with checkJs: true and avoid file renames, but they are verbose, lack features like generics and utility types, and are harder to maintain. Converting to .ts files is the recommended long-term approach.
